title: The Regularization Fix
concept: To prevent forgetting, we can add a penalty (like KL divergence) to the loss function, stopping weights from moving too far from the base model.

exercises.prompt: What happens when an AI model suffers from catastrophic forgetting?

options.text: It learns new things much faster.
It completely forgets its previously learned skills.
It stops learning anything new at all.

exercises.explain: Catastrophic forgetting happens when a model learns new data but completely overwrites its previously learned knowledge.

cards.title: Stop the Forgetfulness!
cards.body: When you teach a smart AI a new trick, it often overwrites its old tricks. This is called 'catastrophic forgetting.' But what if we could add a digital 'leash' that lets the AI learn new things without wandering too far from what it already knows? That is where regularization saves the day!

cards.title: What is Regularization?
cards.body: Regularization is a technique where we add a penalty to the AI's training loss. In fine-tuning, this penalty (often calculated using KL divergence) measures how much the new model's outputs differ from the original base model. If the model changes too much, it gets penalized, forcing it to stay close to its roots.

cards.title: The Anchor Key
cards.body: Think of training an AI like practicing a new song on the piano. If you try to learn a jazz version without any rules, you might completely forget how the original classical piece sounded. Regularization is like keeping one hand lightly resting on the original sheet music, ensuring you don't drift too far from the tune you already know.

cards.title: Chatbots Keeping Their Cool
cards.body: When developers update a chatbot to learn medical terms, they use KL divergence regularization. If the chatbot starts changing its basic grammar rules to fit the medical data, the regularization penalty spikes, forcing the AI to keep its normal language skills intact while still learning the new terms.

cards.title: Do We Lock the Model?
cards.body: People often think that to keep old skills, you must freeze all the model's weights. In reality, you don't need to lock them in place! Regularization lets the weights wiggle and adapt, as long as they don't drift too far from the original base model.

cards.title: Weight Movement Comparison
cards.body: Let's look at how adding a penalty changes how a model's weights move during training.

left.text: Weights drift far away. The AI learns the new task but completely loses its original abilities.

right.text: Weights stay close to the starting point. The AI learns the new task while keeping its old skills.

exercises.prompt: Why do we use regularization like KL divergence when fine-tuning a model?

options.text: To make the model train ten times faster.
To keep the model's new behavior close to its original base knowledge.
To delete useless data from the training set.
To change the model's structure entirely.

exercises.explain: The main purpose of regularization during fine-tuning is to keep the model's behavior close to the original base model, preventing it from forgetting general knowledge.

exercises.prompt: True or False: KL divergence can be used as a penalty to measure how far the fine-tuned model has drifted from the base model.
exercises.explain: KL divergence measures the difference between two distributions, helping us calculate how far the new model has drifted from the base model.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of how we protect old knowledge.
exercises.sentence: To prevent forgetting, we add a regularization [blank] to the loss function.
exercises.explain: A regularization penalty is added to the loss function to discourage the AI from changing its weights too drastically from the original model.

exercises.prompt: Which of these shows a model that desperately needed regularization during its training?

options.text: An AI chatbot perfectly explains a complex science topic while keeping its friendly tone.
An AI trained on medicine starts outputting gibberish because it forgot basic English.
An AI model predicts the weather accurately after being updated with new satellite images.

exercises.explain: The AI response shows the classic sign of catastrophic forgetting (losing basic skills like spelling when learning a new task), which regularization is designed to prevent.

exercises.prompt: How does a regularization penalty like KL divergence actually stop an AI model from forgetting its old skills?

options.text: It adds a penalty to the loss function based on how far the model drifts from the original.
It deletes the new dataset if the model starts to change its weights at all.
It locks all weights completely so the model cannot learn anything new.
It forces the model to double its learning rate every time it makes a mistake.

exercises.explain: Adding a KL divergence penalty to the loss function acts as an anchor, preventing the model from wandering too far from its original pre-trained knowledge while it learns a new task.
